title: EPA Pesticide Devices: A Guide for Consumers

regulation_framework.law: FIFRA (Federal Insecticide, Fungicide and Rodenticide Act)
regulation_framework.device_definition: Any instrument or contrivance (other than a firearm) intended for trapping, destroying, repelling, or mitigating any pest.
regulation_framework.key_distinction: Devices work by physical or mechanical means (e.g., electricity, UV light, filtration) and do not contain a pesticidal substance. Pesticides are substances or mixtures intended to prevent, destroy, repel, or mitigate pests.

must_include: EPA Establishment Number (indicates production facility, NOT product approval)
must_not_include: EPA Registration Number (that would indicate a registered pesticide, not a device)
label_requirements.notes: An EPA Establishment Number on device packaging does NOT indicate the product has been reviewed for safety or efficacy, nor does it imply EPA product approval, registration, certification, or endorsement.

exempt_from_fifra_device_regulation.description: Devices that depend more upon the performance of the user than the device itself
HEPA filters that limit claims to particle size and do not claim to purify air or mitigate microorganisms
Water filters that limit claims to taste, odor, or sediment and do not claim to purify water or mitigate microorganisms
Explicitly excluded from FIFRA device definition; regulated by ATF
examples: Rifles used to control feral hogs

exempt_from_fifra_device_regulation.description: Equipment used to apply pesticides sold and distributed separately from the pesticide itself
Air treatment products that do not claim to purify the air or kill pests; regulated by CPSC instead

regulated_device_categories.description: Claim to kill, inactivate, or suppress growth of microorganisms such as fungi, bacteria, or viruses; also used to attract insects or kill plant pathogens.
examples: UV devices for powdery mildew on strawberries
regulated_device_categories.conditions: Must not contain or be sold with a pesticidal substance (e.g., silver, zinc, copper) to qualify as a device rather than a pesticide.
regulated_device_categories.additional_notes: Some UV light devices used in FDA-regulated healthcare settings may be dually regulated by both EPA and FDA.
references: EPA Compliance Advisory on UV Lights

regulated_device_categories.category: Air Purifiers, Filters, or Air Treatment Devices
regulated_device_categories.description: Claim to reduce microorganisms, unqualified organic contaminants, unqualified allergens, or purify the air.
regulated_device_categories.conditions: Products that create ions from the air are generally regulated as devices unless they contain or are sold with a pesticidal substance or have a pesticidal coating.

subcategories.name: Bipolar Ionization Devices
subcategories.description: Technology generating positively and negatively charged particles; used in HVAC or portable air purifiers.
subcategories.warning: Has potential to generate ozone and other harmful by-products indoors. EPA recommends devices meeting UL 2998 standard (Zero Ozone Emissions certification).
subcategories.standard: UL 2998 (Environmental Claim Validation Procedure for Zero Ozone Emissions from Air Cleaners)

subcategories.name: Photocatalytic Air/Surface Treatment Products
subcategories.description: Use materials with photocatalytic properties (e.g., titanium dioxide) to generate reactive oxygen species.
subcategories.warning: Photocatalytic products in liquid, dust, or coating form are considered pesticides, not devices, and require FIFRA Section 3 registration.

regulated_device_categories.description: Claim to kill, inactivate, or suppress growth of microorganisms including fungi, algae, bacteria, or viruses.
regulated_device_categories.conditions: Considered devices when sold alone. If sold or distributed with a substance (e.g., salt) or output solutions, may be part of a pesticide product requiring registration.
regulated_device_categories.regulatory_notes: EPA issued a tolerance exemption on September 14, 2022 for hypochlorous acid residues on food-contact surfaces in public eating places (Federal Register 2022-19799).
regulated_device_categories.safety_warning: EPA recommends electrolyzed water generated by these devices not be ingested or applied to the human body.

regulated_device_categories.category: Water Purifiers or Water Treatment Units
regulated_device_categories.description: Claim to kill, inactivate, or suppress growth of fungi, algae, bacteria, viruses, or cysts.
examples: Drinking water filter units (microbial removal by physical/mechanical means)
regulated_device_categories.conditions: Must not contain any substance intended to disinfect the water. If they do, they are pesticide products requiring registration.
regulated_device_categories.exclusions: Filters limiting claims to taste, odor, or sediment without purification or microbial mitigation claims are NOT regulated under FIFRA.

regulated_device_categories.description: Claim to kill or entrap insects and similar pests through physical means, including UV light or ozone.
examples: Fly ribbons / fly paper / sticky traps (without attractant)
regulated_device_categories.conditions: Must not be sold with a pesticidal substance to qualify as a device.

regulated_device_categories.description: Claim to control subterranean animals via carbon monoxide generation or subterranean explosion.
regulated_device_categories.conditions: Must be sold or distributed without a substance. If sold with a substance, registration as a pesticide is required.

regulated_device_categories.description: Claim to repel pests such as birds, mice, and underground animals.
examples: Non-lethal bear sound-based deterrents (not regulated as firearm by ATF)

regulated_device_categories.description: Claim to treat insects, mold, and other microorganisms on raw agricultural commodities and other inanimate objects.
regulated_device_categories.conditions: Regulated under FIFRA unless otherwise exempt.

not_devices_commonly_mistaken.reason: A filter that physically traps microbial pests AND incorporates a pesticidal substance that kills them is an antimicrobial pesticide product, not a device.
not_devices_commonly_mistaken.regulation: Requires EPA registration as a pesticide product.

not_devices_commonly_mistaken.reason: Products that generate metal ions (silver, zinc, copper) as the pesticidal agent contain a pesticidal substance and are generally pesticides, not devices.
not_devices_commonly_mistaken.regulation: Requires EPA registration as a pesticide product.

not_devices_commonly_mistaken.product_type: Apparatus sold with a substance to generate a pesticide
not_devices_commonly_mistaken.reason: An apparatus sold with a substance (e.g., salt) used to generate a pesticide is generally considered a pesticide, not a device.
not_devices_commonly_mistaken.regulation: Requires EPA registration as a pesticide product.

not_devices_commonly_mistaken.reason: Products impregnated with ingredients claiming to be 'self-cleaning' or products in liquid, dust, or coating form that claim to kill viruses/bacteria are generally pesticides.
not_devices_commonly_mistaken.regulation: Requires EPA registration as a pesticide product.

not_devices_commonly_mistaken.product_type: Pesticide application equipment sold with a pesticide
not_devices_commonly_mistaken.reason: Application equipment packaged or sold with a registered pesticide must be included in the pesticide registration; it is not classified as a device.
not_devices_commonly_mistaken.regulation: Registered along with the pesticide under 40 C.F.R. § 152.3.

dual_regulation_cases.description: Some products meet both the FIFRA device definition (EPA) and the FFDCA device definition (FDA) and are dually regulated.
examples: Air purifiers in FDA-regulated healthcare settings
UV light devices in FDA-regulated healthcare settings

tips: Use devices consistent with precautionary language and directions for use.
Contact the manufacturer or seller directly with questions on proper product use.
Look for an EPA Establishment Number (not Registration Number) on device packaging.
An EPA Establishment Number does NOT mean the product's safety or efficacy has been reviewed by EPA.
Ask companies applying a pesticidal solution generated on-site for the product's EPA Registration Number.
An unregistered output solution from a device that is a pesticide cannot be sold or distributed without violating FIFRA.

state_regulation_note: Some states impose additional requirements for devices, including registration and efficacy data review before sale. Compliance with federal FIFRA does not guarantee legal sale in all states. See AAPCO (https://aapco.org) for state lead agency contacts.
